vulnerability.title: Prevent remote hosts from connecting to the proxy display
vulnerability.description: The SSH daemon should prevent remote hosts from connecting to the proxy display. The default SSH configuration for X11UseLocalhost is yes, which prevents remote hosts from connecting to the proxy display. To explicitly prevent remote connections to the proxy display, add or correct the following line in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf: X11UseLocalhost yes
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*X11UseLocalhost\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config" LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*X11UseLocalhost\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"/*.conf if [ -e "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" ] ; then LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*X11UseLocalhost\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" else touch "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" fi # make sure file has newline at the end sed -i -e '$a\' "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/0

triage_decision.reason: Setting X11UseLocalhost to yes is a standard SSH configuration change that does not affect authentication or authorization beyond preventing remote X11 forwarding. It follows STIG hardening patterns and is unlikely to cause lockout or system breakage.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to do PLAN step: describe plan.

We need to modify /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf to include "X11UseLocalhost yes". Or maybe target specific file. Let's read that file. Maybe file exists. Use read_file. We need to ensure file exists. First step: check if /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf exists and open. If not, create the file. Plan: Use read_file to see contents. Then use sed to delete any existing X11UseLocalhost lines. Then append the line if not present. Use sed to modify in-place: need to avoid duplicate. We can use sed to uncomment if commented. If file not exist, create with content. But simpler: if file exists, run sed to replace or add. But we cannot use a command with condition chaining. We can run separate commands.

Plan: 1) Check existence: run_cmd 'test -f /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf' might return exit code. For planning we just mention we will create if missing.

We need to follow plan: read the file. Then if does not contain X11UseLocalhost, add. And ensure it's yes. Use sed to modify if present: match ^#?\s*X11UseLocalhost.* and replace with X11UseLocalhost yes. If not found, append with echo. But echo can't be done via tools? We use write_file: get file content read_file, then append the line and write back. To avoid modifying only part, we can read the file content, check lines. If not contains a line starting with X11UseLocalhost, then write back with appended line.

So plan: read_file /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf. If not exist, can get error, but we can proceed to create. Instead we could just write file with that line. But spec may not exist. But best to create with the line. Might need to pre-create directory /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d.

We will write file: "X11UseLocalhost yes" content.

Thus plan: create config file if missing or edit it.

But we need to be cautious: we don't want duplicate. We follow above approach.

We should also reload sshd after change, but requirement doesn't state to reload. But usually required. But not part of finding? Usually need to restart sshd. Maybe not necessary for scan? But typical remediation may include systemctl reload sshd. But instructions didn't forbid. But we maybe should.

Given number of commands we can use. After change, reload service. Use run_cmd 'systemctl reload sshd'. That one command.

Let's produce plan with 2 commands: create file & reload.

We should also use sed -i to modify, but easier to write file content. Let's produce plan.

We must use review_plan to request approval.
